title: oxalis diamantinae
intro: Oxalis diamantinae, also known as the Diamantina Wood Sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae) that is native to Brazil. The plant contains oxalic acid, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat skin conditions, such as acne and eczema, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Oxalis diamantinae include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic. Oxalis diamantinae is typically prepared as a topical cream or salve, and can also be ingested as a tea or capsule, although its safety and efficacy as an oral treatment have not been extensively studied.
